current_active_queue.label: Real SDK Vertical Slice And Canonical Runtime Evidence
current_active_queue.why_now: Local AI Runtime keeps the same active queue after the strategic return. The repo now accepts compliant AgentBridge markdown task intake on host_local through a fail-closed adapter, completes a repo-side projection parity loop from markdown task to result.json plus markdown projection, and still keeps result.json as formal evidence with AgentBridge markdown as compatibility projection. With host_local crash recovery, lease helpers, exec fallback process-guard, the minimal verification runner, the safe intake adapter, a repo-owned host_local task entrypoint plus worker factory support for `codex_sdk / codex_exec`, repo-side parity, a live planner-sidecar receipt boundary, the repo-side review gate, repo-side predicate coverage plus force-on overrides, the git-backed write-boundary enforcement, the minimal worktree manager, the cleanup manager, the runtime-backed `dispatch_state.json` ledger, repo-side `stale / cancelled / resumed / retry` lifecycle helpers, repo-side `planner_result.json` receipts, repo-side `review_result.json` receipts, repo-side `closeout_bundle.json` receipts, repo-side `leases / route / quota` closeout, deterministic multi-worker simulation, remote_non_gui promotion evidence, a repo-owned Hermes parity / historical snapshot mapping verifier, vm_gui conditional promotion evidence, a bounded live heterogeneous review sidecar receipt on configured host_local review paths, and an explicit E-T01 decision to keep `compatibility_projection_ref` and `lane` unchanged while non-host_local runner wiring is still open, the repo-side topology promotion proof is complete while the next bounded execution sequence turns to non-host_local runner wiring plus any follow-on review hardening and the selector stays at promote_phase1_execution. In parallel, Kernel V2 dual-track bootstrap is now absorbed into code, config, tests, docs, and verifier surfaces, but the default entrypoint is still v1 and the active queue remains unchanged.

current_live_posture.summary: Local AI Runtime currently executes repo-side through canonical normalization on host_local, writes result.json as formal evidence, writes `.ai/runs/<run_id>/<task_id>/dispatch_state.json` as the runtime ledger companion, writes `planner_result.json` on live planner-sidecar paths, writes `review_result.json` on review-gated paths, writes `closeout_bundle.json` on current planner/review/completed runtime outcomes, and keeps AgentBridge results as compatibility projection. The execution hot path is Codex-first on `host_local`, while Hermes stays in the repo truth as the risk/ledger/cross-executor sidecar and Claude now materializes a bounded live review sidecar receipt on configured host_local review paths. host_local crash recovery, minimal lease lifecycle helpers, exec fallback process-tree guarding, the minimal verification runner, a fail-closed AgentBridge markdown intake adapter, a repo-owned host_local task entrypoint plus worker factory support for `codex_sdk / codex_exec`, repo-side projection parity, a live planner-sidecar receipt boundary, a repo-side review gate, repo-side predicate coverage plus force-on overrides, git-backed write-boundary enforcement, a minimal worktree manager, a cleanup manager, the runtime-backed dispatch ledger, repo-side lifecycle helpers, repo-side route selection plus lease quota handoff guard, deterministic multi-worker simulation, remote_non_gui promotion evidence, vm_gui conditional promotion evidence, repo-side structured receipts, and a repo-owned Hermes parity suite are now landed. The host_local task entrypoint currently materializes `host-orchestrator --run-task` and `run-host-task.ps1` from repo-owned worker profiles, directly exercises `local_maint` through `codex_sdk`, keeps built-in `codex_exec` profiles on the non-host-local handoff boundary until those lanes are really wired, and keeps `scripted / gpt54_direct / claude_glm` fail-closed for live primary task execution rather than pretending they already run. The Hermes parity suite materializes the certified baseline doc, the current known-good / boundary anchors, the snapshot contract, the known-good validator, and the historical container lifecycle boundary into one rerunnable summary; in the current shell only the env-sensitive `independent_key / independent_base_url` bring-up gates remain intentionally red. The repo now also has a remote_non_gui promotion suite that replays both default local-lane handoff and explicit remote-profile fail-closed handoff across 2 scenarios while keeping leases drained and workers idle, plus a vm_gui promotion suite that replays both default GUI-only handoff and explicit `vm_gui_probe` fail-closed handoff across 2 scenarios while keeping leases drained and workers idle; both are repo-side promotion proofs, not live remote/vm execution or `live accepted`. The current repo-side closeout also keeps the E-T01 field-name boundary: `compatibility_projection_ref` and `lane` stay unchanged until non-host_local runner wiring and any follow-on review stability work are both real. Planner-gated tasks currently run a codex-backed live planner sidecar on eligible host_local profiles, write `planner_result.json`, and still stop at `waiting_handoff` before any primary worker execution; review-gated tasks currently stop at `needs_review` after worker plus verification and before downstream flow while also writing `review_result.json`; when a configured `review_worker_profile` has bounded primary worker output summary, the runtime now runs a `claude_glm_review` sidecar in `--bare --no-session-persistence` mode from an isolated temp cwd and writes a live heterogeneous review receipt, and when that sidecar cannot materialize it falls back fail-closed to a repo-side blocking receipt. Declared isolated worktree tasks now fail closed on repo-escape path claims, worktree root drift, branch drift, and post-run writes outside `allowed_paths` or inside `forbidden_paths` when the workspace has a `.git` admin path; worktree isolation currently covers writes only and does not imply memory/provider/session isolation. Runtime-managed clean linked worktrees now auto-remove after a successful non-handoff run, while review-pending, failed, dirty, or externally launched isolated worktrees stay deferred with an auditable `worktree_cleanup` event, and unexpected remove errors surface as `cleanup_failed`. `result.json` now carries `cleanup_owner`, `status_reason`, `dispatch_state_ref`, `planner_result_ref`, `review_result_ref`, `closeout_bundle_ref`, and `route_reason`, while `dispatch_state.json` and `route_decisions` materialize the same repo-owned route choice. Canonical tasks may now explicitly select a repo-owned `worker_profile`; when omitted, the orchestrator default profile fills it, and selected profile `max_active_leases` exhaustion now fails closed to pre-worker handoff rather than pretending a multi-worker scheduler is already live. Repo-side lifecycle ops can now materialize `cancelled / stale / resumed`; explicit `cancel / resume / retry` clear active leases, while `resume / retry` refresh `heartbeat_at / stale_after`, and `retry` increments `attempt` while recording `retry_rewind`; `queued / input_required` still remain reserved. Branch deletion still remains out of scope. `user_forced_planner / user_forced_review` now act as force-on-only overrides rather than documentation-only fields. Hermes -> AgentBridge -> Codex remains the product mainline and migration target; live planner sidecar currently materializes only on codex-backed host_local profiles, bounded live heterogeneous review receipts currently materialize only from runtime status plus primary worker output summary on configured host_local review paths, non-host_local runners are still not wired, execution-critical markdown overrides remain intentionally unsupported, and these repo-side planner/review/path-guard/worktree/cleanup/ledger/route/quota/simulation/promotion/receipt claims do not yet mean live `Direct GPT-5.4` planner, live `Claude Code + GLM-5.2` primary task execution, platform compatibility green, or live accepted. The GPT-5.4 gateway probe and the codex exec minimum probe are ready; network_proxy remains platform_na, so current live execution should still stay within pure local task boundaries. Kernel V2 dual-track bootstrap is now a repo-side truth surface with its own `control-plane-v2.db` and `.ai/runs-v2/<run_id>/<task_id>/<attempt_id>/` artifacts, but the default entrypoint is still v1 until cutover conditions are met.

certified_baseline.label: Hermes AgentBridge Historical Baseline And Strategic Reference
certified_baseline.claim_boundary: Hermes/AgentBridge remains the certified historical baseline and strategic north-star reference for Local AI Runtime as a three-layer product line. Current repo runtime truth stays on canonical internal normalization plus result.json evidence and compatibility projection, with direct host_local markdown intake now wired under repo-owned defaults and fail-closed override rejection.
